_comment: Channel Limits Database - safety limits and verification for runtime write validation. This file is a pure projection of the namespace-union manifest (osprey.services.virtual_accelerator.manifest): it carries exactly one entry per manifest address, and the write-safety contract is a single rule -- a channel is writable if and only if it is a setpoint (:SP). The 168 :SP addresses are writable with min/max bounds (family-banded from the SR lattice model's device inventory and machine.json nominal values); every other manifest address (readbacks :RB / :X / :Y, status and fault flags, golden references, telemetry) is writable:false so the software safety layer -- not just the IOC -- refuses writes to it. Regenerate with the manifest; tests/templates/test_channel_limits_va.py guards the projection.
_description: This database configures automatic limits validation and verification for channel writes. The connector reads it automatically - capabilities need not know these details. max_step is intentionally NOT set on any entry: LimitsValidator's max_step check reads the current value via a direct pyepics caget regardless of connector type (not connector-abstracted), which would block every write under the default mock control-system type (no live EPICS server behind mock addresses). min/max bounds plus readback verification already enforce 'a value must fit inside its own limits' without that failure mode.

defaults._comment: Default settings inherited by all channels unless overridden

verification._comment: Default verification: callback (fast, EPICS IOC confirmation)
